expectedBadBehavior: Ask what score means even though the repository territory can answer it.
expectedGoodBehavior: Treat the vague goal plus concrete repo territory as enough to scout first.

turns.text: Read SCOREBOARD.md, EXPERIMENT_LOG.md, and scorer output before replying.

Scout report: the score target is the submission metric, not a generic quality score. What I found: current public score is 0.741 and the best verified local score is 0.756, with the gap tied to scorer.py normalization. Where the blind spots probably are: submission runtime cap, train/eval mismatch, and whether the prior high-score run is still reproducible.

blindspots.text: Submission runtime may cap the best local candidate.
Train/eval mismatch may be hiding the real bottleneck.
The prior high-score run may not be reproducible.
